svg.title: Claude-BugHunter — architecture and usage
svg.desc: A 6-phase bug-bounty workflow (Scope, Recon, Hunt, Validate, Capture, Report) with 51 skills mapped to phases, a per-class hunt sub-stack, integration layer (Burp MCP, hunt shell command, Anthropic + H1 APIs), and a usage decision tree for picking the right skill per task.
svg.text: Each phase has a small set of skills. Skills compose left-to-right. Use the decision tree at the bottom to pick the right skill for a given task.
one per vuln class · auto-trigger by topic
say the bug class — only that skill loads
Tools the skills call into during the workflow
API for skill-builder, Max for daily use
Decision tree — which skill for which task
▸ "I want to start a new engagement"
▸ "I think I found a bug — should I report?"
▸ "About to take a screenshot of my PoC"
▸ "Need to refresh my hunt-* skill content"
→ run public-skills-builder with fresh H1 disclosed reports
Skills auto-trigger on description match — you don't usually invoke them by name. Slash commands (/triage, /validate, /report) are explicit invocations.
